name: testing-qa
description: Comprehensive testing and QA workflow covering unit testing, integration testing, E2E testing, browser automation, and quality assurance.
capabilities: Design comprehensive testing strategies across multiple testing phases
Guide implementation of unit, integration, and E2E tests
Provide skill recommendations for different testing needs
Set up browser automation and visual regression testing
Define quality gates and code review processes
Configure performance testing and load testing workflows
author: sickn33
securitySummary: This skill is a documentation-only markdown file describing a testing and QA workflow bundle. The static analyzer flagged backtick syntax in markdown as potential shell commands, but this is a false positive. The file contains no executable code, no network requests, and no system access. All 67 detected patterns are false positives from markdown documentation.
valueStatement: This skill provides a structured testing and QA workflow covering unit tests, integration tests, E2E testing, browser automation, and quality gates for production-ready software.
userTitle: Set Up Comprehensive Testing QA Workflow
limitations: This skill is a documentation bundle, not an executable tool
Does not directly run tests - provides guidance only
Requires other skills to execute specific testing tasks
Does not include test frameworks or libraries

useCases.title: Set Up Testing Infrastructure
useCases.description: Use this workflow to establish a comprehensive testing strategy for a new project, including unit tests, integration tests, and E2E tests.

useCases.title: Automate Browser Testing
useCases.description: Implement browser automation and visual regression testing for web applications using Playwright and screenshot tools.

useCases.title: Establish Quality Gates
useCases.description: Define quality gates with linting, code review, security scanning, and verification steps before production deployment.

promptTemplates.title: Design Testing Strategy
promptTemplates.prompt: Use @testing-qa to design a comprehensive testing strategy for my project. I need to cover unit tests, integration tests, and E2E tests.
promptTemplates.scenario: When starting a new project and need to establish testing infrastructure

promptTemplates.title: Implement Unit Tests
promptTemplates.prompt: Use @testing-qa to help me write unit tests. I want to cover JavaScript with Jest and Python with pytest.
promptTemplates.scenario: When writing unit tests for application code

promptTemplates.title: Create E2E Tests
promptTemplates.prompt: Use @testing-qa to create end-to-end tests using Playwright. I need to test critical user paths.

promptTemplates.title: Set Up Quality Gates
promptTemplates.prompt: Use @testing-qa to define quality gates for my project. Include linting, code review, security scanning, and test coverage requirements.

bestPractices: Follow the testing pyramid: prioritize unit tests (70%) over integration (20%) and E2E (10%)
Use quality gates to enforce test coverage thresholds before deployment
Automate visual regression testing to catch UI changes automatically
antiPatterns: Skipping unit tests in favor of only E2E tests (slow and brittle)
Setting unrealistic test coverage targets without considering test maintenance
Ignoring security scanning in the quality gates

faq.question: What is the testing pyramid?
faq.answer: The testing pyramid is a concept that guides test distribution: 70% unit tests (fast, focused), 20% integration tests (service interactions), and 10% E2E tests (critical user paths).

faq.question: Does this skill run tests?
faq.answer: No, this is a workflow documentation skill that provides guidance and references to other skills that can execute tests.

faq.question: What testing frameworks are supported?
faq.answer: The workflow references JavaScript (Jest/Vitest), Python (pytest), Playwright for E2E, and various other testing tools.

faq.question: How do I integrate with CI/CD?
faq.answer: Use the quality gates phase to configure linting, test execution, security scanning, and verification steps in your CI pipeline.

faq.question: What are quality gates?
faq.answer: Quality gates are checkpoints that must pass before code proceeds, including test coverage, linting, security scans, and code review approval.

faq.question: Can I use this for performance testing?
faq.answer: Yes, Phase 6 covers performance testing with references to performance engineering and profiling skills.
